name: OLD LUMBER COMPANY GRILL AND BAR

violations.description: Preventing contamination during storage. — --Food item was not stored in a clean, dry location a minimum of 6 inches above the floor. Multiple food items were found on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food item should be stored off of the floor.

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --In-use utensil was not properly stored during pauses in food preparation or dispensing. Scoop for ice was found in a pan with dirty water. Ice scoops should be stored in a clean location between uses.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Reach-in coolers and freezers were found dirty, inside and out. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Multiple single-use items were found stored on the floor of the downstairs storage area. Single-use items should be stored off of the floor.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Basement floor was found dirty. This should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Duties of person in charge. — --Person in charge did not ensure employee compliance with an aspect of the food service operation specified in the food service code. When asked how hot the potatoes reheating were going to be heated, the cook replied 150F. When asked how long he had to fully heat the potatoes, he replied 2 hours. Potentially hazardous food items should be reheated to 165F with-in 1 hour. Person in charge failed to make sure employees know the proper reheating procedures.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Cut tomato in the refrigerated prep table was found at 50F. Ice build up can be seen on the inside of the prep table. Refrigerated prep table should be properly maintained so potentially hazardous foods can be properly stored at 41F or below. ***Items discarded.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. Deli meats found in the reach-in cooler without dates. Potentially hazardous foods should be date marked when held for more than 24 hours.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Reach-in coolers and freezers were found dirty, inside and out. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.--Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. Filters for the hood ventilation systems were found dirty. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Surface of equipment/utensil did not reach minimum temperature of 160F during hot water sanitize rinse of warewash machine. When dish machine was tested during inspection, inspector's thermometer did not reach 160F after running 3 cycles in the machine. Dish machines using heat for sanitizing should heat equipment surfaces to at least 160F.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. Multiple single-use items were found stored on the floor of the downstairs storage area. Single-use items should be stored off of the floor.

Removal frequency of refuse, recyclables, and returnables. — --Refuse, recyclables, and returnables were not removed from the premises at a frequency to minimize odors and conditions that attract insects and rodents. Large pile of cardboard boxes was found in the downstairs storage area. Boxes should be removed more frequently.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --Employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. Employee food items, yogurt, leftovers, and jar of home canned salsa, found stored on the top shelf of the Red Bull cooler in the kitchen. Employee food items should be stored separate from items used by facility for food service.
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
Clean/dry location, protected, covered, original containers, labeled (2 pts deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
Provided as required, designed, constructed, installed, maintained, and cleaned (1 pt deducted)
